mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers discount rates and refinancing stress, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than baseline when FEDFUNDS is declining, or if walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 2-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and demand, which should pressure near-term corporate earnings and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with a rising UNRATE are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the effect reverses for any sustained 18-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rebound in retail sales and industrial production indicates broad demand acceleration that supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive months in RSAFS and INDPRO do not outperform single-series signals, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-acceleration observations.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is accelerating while housing starts are also rising, the combination signals improving real activity and capacity utilization, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rise on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in INDPRO and HOUST is not associated with higher 60-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the sign is negative in more than 45% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate constrains financial conditions and compresses equity multiples, so SPX should underperform over the next 20 trading days when policy is above a restrictive threshold.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX posts positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or if the conditional effect is absent after controlling for the prior 3-month rate trend.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDP and retail sales signals weakening final demand, which should reduce expected earnings and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment regime supports wage income and consumption, which sustains equity demand and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low-UNRATE periods are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the signal loses predictive value after the first month of a low-unemployment regime.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises while unemployment falls, the economy is simultaneously gaining output and labor demand, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint improving pattern in INDPRO and UNRATE does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered episodes fail to deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations.
